title: viola hirta subsp. thomasiana
intro: Viola hirta subsp. thomasiana, also known as Thomas's hairy violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Viola hirta subsp. thomasiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
